title: Handling Slang and Context
concept: Translation models must adapt to cultural idioms and context; literal translations often fail. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Text Classification with Transformers.'

options.text: Translating only when the user is speaking.

exercises.explain: Literal translation means translating word-for-word, which often ruins the true meaning of idioms and slang.

cards.title: Lost in Translation?
cards.body: If you translate 'piece of cake' literally into another language, people might wonder where the dessert is! How does AI learn that we actually mean something is super easy? Let's find out!

cards.title: What is Contextual Translation?
cards.body: Contextual translation goes beyond matching word-for-word. It looks at surrounding sentences, cultural background, and modern slang to capture the true, intended meaning of a phrase.

cards.title: The Formal Dinner Guest
cards.body: Imagine a translation model as a formal dinner guest trying to understand street slang. If someone says 'break a leg,' the guest might panic and call an ambulance instead of wishing them good luck! Just like a guest needs to learn local expressions, AI needs contextual training to understand we don't mean literal broken bones.

cards.title: Slang in Action
cards.body: If someone texts 'That movie was fire 🔥', a literal translator might warn of a fire hazard. A context-aware model understands the slang and translates it as 'That movie was excellent.'

cards.title: The Dictionary Myth
cards.body: Many people believe that translation is just a massive digital dictionary matching word-for-word. In reality, literal translation often completely ruins the meaning. AI must look at the whole picture to translate accurately.

cards.title: Literal vs. Contextual
cards.body: See how translating word-by-word changes the message compared to analyzing the whole context.

left.text: 'Break a leg' -> Snaps your bones physically.

right.text: 'Break a leg' -> Good luck on your performance!

exercises.prompt: What is the main problem with translating idioms like 'bite the bullet' literally?

options.text: It makes the AI translate faster.
It ignores cultural meaning, leading to confusing translations.
It only works for mathematical formulas.

exercises.explain: Literal translations fail to capture the cultural meaning of idioms, making the output sound confusing or robotic.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An AI translator needs context because the same word can have different meanings in different sentences.
exercises.explain: Context is essential because words change meaning based on how, when, and where they are used.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of AI translation:
exercises.sentence: To translate slang correctly, the AI must look at the surrounding words to find the right context.
exercises.explain: Without context, a translation model might think 'cool' always refers to cold weather, instead of meaning 'trendy' or 'okay'.

exercises.prompt: An AI is translating the English slang phrase 'I'm feeling blue' into another language. Spot the output that successfully translates the context instead of the literal words:

options.text: AI: 'I am feeling quite sad today.'
AI: 'I am experiencing the color blue on my skin.'

exercises.explain: Option A sounds like a real, context-aware translation of the slang 'feeling blue'. Option B is a robotic, literal translation. Option C is just random words.

exercises.prompt: How can we best help a translation AI understand that 'spill the beans' means to reveal a secret, rather than dropping actual food?

options.text: Translate every slang word literally to maintain accuracy.
Ignore slang entirely and remove it from the input text.
Train the AI on diverse, real-world conversations to learn context.
Replace all sentences with simple dictionary definitions.

exercises.explain: Excellent work! You've mastered how AI decodes human slang and context. Next up: Text Classification with Transformers.
